import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer
import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable

/**
 * The representation of a specific civil date and time without a reference to a particular time zone.
 *
 * This class does not describe specific *moments in time*, which are represented as [Instant] values.
 * Instead, its instances can be thought of as clock readings, something that an observer in a particular time zone
 * could witness.
 * For example, `2020-08-30T18:43` is not a *moment in time*, since someone in Berlin and someone in Tokyo would witness
 * this on their clocks at different times.
 *
 * The main purpose of this class is to provide human-readable representations of [Instant] values, or to transfer them
 * as data.
 *
 * The arithmetic on [LocalDateTime] values is not provided, since without accounting for the time zone transitions it may give misleading results.
 *
 * ### Acquiring [LocalDateTime] instances
 *
 * - [Instant.toLocalDateTime] converts an [Instant] to a [LocalDateTime] in the given [TimeZone].
 * - [LocalDateTime.parse] parses an ISO-8601 string (e.g. `2023-01-02T22:35:01`).
 * - The [LocalDateTime] constructor creates an instance from individual date/time components.
 * - Combining [LocalDate] and [LocalTime] with `LocalDateTime(date, time)`,
 *   [LocalDate.atTime], or [LocalTime.atDate].
 *
 * ### Converting to other types
 *
 * - [LocalDateTime.toInstant] converts to an [Instant] given a [TimeZone] or [UtcOffset].
 * - [LocalDateTime.date] and [LocalDateTime.time] extract the date and time parts respectively.
 * - [LocalDateTime.toString] returns the ISO-8601 string representation.
 *
 * ### When to use [LocalDateTime]
 *
 * Use [LocalDateTime] in two primary scenarios:
 * - To represent events scheduled far in the future at a local time (like a meeting in a few months).
 *   Keep track of the [TimeZone] of the event separately. Avoid converting future events to [Instant] in advance,
 *   because time zone rules may change unexpectedly.
 * - To display or format an [Instant] for a user in their local time zone.
 *
 * For timestamps of events that have already happened, prefer [Instant].
 *
 * ### Pitfalls of [LocalDateTime] comparison
 *
 * Because [LocalDateTime] does not carry time zone information, comparing two [LocalDateTime] values
 * only makes sense when they are in the same time zone. Even then, during fall-back daylight saving time transitions,
 * a "lesser" [LocalDateTime] may actually correspond to a later moment in time than a "greater" one.
 *
 * @see LocalDate for the date part.
 * @see LocalTime for the time part.
 * @see Instant for representing specific moments in time.

         * // `08/30 18:43:13`, using a custom format:

import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalTimeIso8601Serializer

 *   and then [LocalDateTime.time] extracts the time part.
 * - [LocalTime.parse] parses an ISO-8601 string (e.g. `12:30:00`).
 * - The [LocalTime] constructor creates an instance from hour, minute, second, and nanosecond components.
 * - [LocalTime.fromSecondOfDay], [LocalTime.fromMillisecondOfDay], and [LocalTime.fromNanosecondOfDay] create
 *   an instance from the time elapsed since midnight.

 * ### Converting [LocalTime] to other types

 * - [LocalTime.atDate] and [LocalDate.atTime] combine a time with a date to produce a [LocalDateTime].
 * - [LocalTime.toSecondOfDay], [LocalTime.toMillisecondOfDay], and [LocalTime.toNanosecondOfDay] return
 *   the time as a numeric offset since midnight.
 * - [LocalTime.toString] returns the ISO-8601 string representation.

 * ### When to use [LocalTime]
 *

     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalTime] from a string.
     * @see LocalTime.format for formatting using a custom format.

    public override fun toString(): String
}

/**
 * Formats this value using the given [format].
 * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
 */
public fun LocalTime.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>): String = format.format(this)

/**
 * @suppress
 */
@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("LocalTime.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
public fun String.toLocalTime(): LocalTime = LocalTime.parse(this)

/**
 * Combines this time's components with the specified date components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
 */
public fun LocalTime.atDate(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
    LocalDateTime(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)

/**
 * Combines this time's components with the specified date components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
 */
public fun LocalTime.atDate(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
    LocalDateTime(year, month, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)

/**
 * Combines this time's components with the specified [LocalDate] components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
 */
public fun LocalTime.atDate(date: LocalDate): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(date, this)

internal fun getIsoTimeFormat() = LocalTime.Formats.ISO

{"file_path": "/project/core/common/src/UtcOffset.kt", "content": "/*

package kotlinx.datetime

import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.UtcOffsetSerializer
import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable

/**
 * An offset from UTC.
 *
 * Represents the amount of time that a local date/time in a given time zone is ahead of or behind UTC.
 *
 * Examples of these values:
 * - `Z`, an offset of zero;
 * - `+05`, plus five hours;
 * - `-02`, minus two hours;
 * - `+03:30`, plus three hours and thirty minutes;
 * - `+01:23:45`, plus one hour, 23 minutes, and 45 seconds.
 *
 * ### Acquiring [UtcOffset] instances
 *
 * - [UtcOffset.ZERO] returns the zero offset (`Z`).
 * - The [UtcOffset] factory function creates an offset from hours, minutes, and seconds components.
 * - [UtcOffset.parse] parses a string in the ISO-8601 offset format (e.g. `+05:30`).
 * - [Instant.offsetIn] returns the offset of a specific time zone at a given instant.
 * - [TimeZone.offsetAt] returns the offset of a time zone at a given instant.
 * - For a [FixedOffsetTimeZone], the [FixedOffsetTimeZone.offset] property provides the constant offset.
 *
 * ### Usage example
 *
 * ```
 * val offset = UtcOffset(hours = 5, minutes = 30)
 * println(offset) // +05:30
 * val localDateTime = LocalDateTime(2023, 1, 1, 0, 0)
 * val instant = localDateTime.toInstant(offset)
 * ```
 *
 * ### Relationship with [TimeZone]
 *
 * A [UtcOffset] is a fixed numeric offset from UTC, while a [TimeZone] can have varying offsets due to
 * daylight saving time transitions. A [UtcOffset] can be converted to a fixed-offset [TimeZone] using
 * [UtcOffset.asTimeZone], which returns a [FixedOffsetTimeZone].
 *
 * The valid range of offsets is `+18:00` to `-18:00`.
 *
 * @see TimeZone for time zones that may have varying offsets.
 * @see FixedOffsetTimeZone for time zones with a constant offset.
 */
@Serializable(with = UtcOffsetSerializer::class)
public expect class UtcOffset {
    /**
     * The number of seconds from UTC.
     *
     * The larger the value, the earlier some specific civil date/time happens with the offset.
     */
    public val totalSeconds: Int

    // TODO: Declare and document toString/equals/hashCode

    public companion object {
        /**
         * The zero offset from UTC, `Z`.
         */
        public val ZERO: UtcOffset

        /**
         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
         *
         * Parses a string that represents a UTC offset and returns the parsed [UtcOffset] value.
         *
         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
         *
         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [UtcOffset] are
         * exceeded.
         */
        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset> = getIsoUtcOffestFormat()): UtcOffset

        /**
         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
         *
         * Example:
         * ```
         * // `GMT` on zero, `+4:30:15`, using a custom format:
         * UtcOffset.Format {
         *   optional("GMT") {
         *     offsetHours(Padding.NONE); char(':'); offsetMinutesOfHour()
         *     optional { char(':'); offsetSecondsOfMinute() }
         *   }
         * }
         * ```
         *
         * Since [UtcOffset] values are rarely formatted and parsed on their own,
         * instances of [DateTimeFormat] obtained here will likely need to be passed to
         * [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.offset] in a format builder for a larger data structure.
         *
         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [UtcOffset.Formats].
         *
         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if parsing using this format is ambiguous.
         */
        @Suppress("FunctionName")
        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
    }

    /**
     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
     *
     * See [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO], [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC], and [UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS]
     * for popular predefined formats.
     *
     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [UtcOffset.Format] to create a custom
     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [UtcOffset] values.
     */
    public object Formats {
        /**
         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [UtcOffset.parse] and [UtcOffset.toString].
         *
         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
         *
         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
         *
         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 format:
         * - `Z` or `+00:00`, an offset of zero;
         * - `+05:00`, five hours;
         * - `-02:00`, minus two hours;
         * - `-17:16`
         * - `+10:36:30`
         */
        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>

        /**
         * ISO 8601 basic format.
         *
         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
         *
         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
         *
         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 basic format:
         * - `Z`
         * - `+05`
         * - `-1716`
         * - `+103630`
         *
         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS
         */
        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>

        /**
         * A subset of the ISO 8601 basic format that always outputs and parses exactly a numeric sign and four digits:
         * two digits for the hour and two digits for the minute. If the offset has a non-zero number of seconds,
         * they are truncated.
         *
         * Examples of UTC offsets in this format:
         * - `+0000`
         * - `+0500`
         * - `-1716`
         * - `+1036`
         *
         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC
         */
        public val FOUR_DIGITS: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
    }

    /**
     * Converts this UTC offset to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
     *
     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [UtcOffset] from a string.
     * @see UtcOffset.format for formatting using a custom format.
     */
    public override fun toString(): String
}

/**
 * Formats this value using the given [format].
 * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
 */
public fun UtcOffset.format(format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>): String = format.format(this)

/**
 * Constructs a [UtcOffset] from hours, minutes, and seconds components.
 *
 * All components must have the same sign.
 *
 * The bounds are checked: it is invalid to pass something other than `+/-[0; 59]` as the number of seconds or minutes.
 * For example, `UtcOffset(hours = 3, minutes = 61)` is invalid.
 *
 * However, the non-null component of the highest order can exceed these bounds,
 * for example, `UtcOffset(minutes = 241)` is valid.
 *
 * @throws IllegalArgumentException if a component exceeds its bounds when a higher order component is specified.
 * @throws IllegalArgumentException if components have different signs.
 * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the resulting `UtcOffset` value is outside of range `+/-18:00`.
 */
public expect fun UtcOffset(hours: Int? = null, minutes: Int? = null, seconds: Int? = null): UtcOffset

@Deprecated("Use UtcOffset.ZERO instead", ReplaceWith("UtcOffset.ZERO"), DeprecationLevel.ERROR)
public fun UtcOffset(): UtcOffset = UtcOffset.ZERO

/**
 * Returns the fixed-offset time zone with the given UTC offset.
 */
public fun UtcOffset.asTimeZone(): FixedOffsetTimeZone = FixedOffsetTimeZone(this)

// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
internal fun getIsoUtcOffestFormat() = UtcOffset.Formats.ISO
"}

  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.

  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer
 11→import kotlin.math.*
 12→import kotlin.time.Duration
 13→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 14→
 15→/**
 16→ * A difference between two [instants][Instant], decomposed into date and time components.
 17→ *
 18→ * The date components are: [years], [months], [days].
 19→ *
 20→ * The time components are: [hours], [minutes], [seconds], [nanoseconds].
 21→ *
 22→ * A `DateTimePeriod` can be constructed using the same-named constructor function,
 23→ * [parsed][DateTimePeriod.parse] from a string, or returned as the result of instant arithmetic operations (see [Instant.periodUntil]).
 24→ * All these functions can return a [DatePeriod] value, which is a subtype of `DateTimePeriod`,
 25→ * a special case that only stores date components, if all time components of the result happen to be zero.
 26→ */
 27→@Serializable(with = DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
 28→// TODO: could be error-prone without explicitly named params
 29→public sealed class DateTimePeriod {
 30→    internal abstract val totalMonths: Int
 31→
 32→    /**
 33→     * The number of calendar days.
 34→     *
 35→     * Note that a calendar day is not identical to 24 hours, see [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
 36→     */
 37→    public abstract val days: Int
 38→    internal abstract val totalNanoseconds: Long
 39→
 40→    /**
 41→     * The number of whole years.
 42→     */
 43→    public val years: Int get() = totalMonths / 12
 44→
 45→    /**
 46→     * The number of months in this period that don't form a whole year, so this value is always in `(-11..11)`.
 47→     */
 48→    public val months: Int get() = totalMonths % 12
 49→
 50→    /**
 51→     * The number of whole hours in this period.
 52→     */
 53→    public open val hours: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds / 3_600_000_000_000).toInt()
 54→
 55→    /**
 56→     * The number of whole minutes in this period that don't form a whole hour, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
 57→     */
 58→    public open val minutes: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 3_600_000_000_000) / 60_000_000_000).toInt()
 59→
 60→    /**
 61→     * The number of whole seconds in this period that don't form a whole minute, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
 62→     */
 63→    public open val seconds: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 60_000_000_000) / NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
 64→
 65→    /**
 66→     * The number of whole nanoseconds in this period that don't form a whole second, so this value is always in
 67→     * `(-999_999_999..999_999_999)`.
 68→     */
 69→    public open val nanoseconds: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
 70→
 71→    private fun allNonpositive() =
 72→        totalMonths <= 0 && days <= 0 && totalNanoseconds <= 0 && (totalMonths or days != 0 || totalNanoseconds != 0L)
 73→
 74→    /**
 75→     * Converts this period to the ISO-8601 string representation for durations.
 76→     *
 77→     * @see DateTimePeriod.parse
 78→     */
 79→    override fun toString(): String = buildString {
 80→        val sign = if (allNonpositive()) { append('-'); -1 } else 1
 81→        append('P')
 82→        if (years != 0) append(years * sign).append('Y')
 83→        if (months != 0) append(months * sign).append('M')
 84→        if (days != 0) append(days * sign).append('D')
 85→        var t = "T"
 86→        if (hours != 0) append(t).append(hours * sign).append('H').also { t = "" }
 87→        if (minutes != 0) append(t).append(minutes * sign).append('M').also { t = "" }
 88→        if (seconds or nanoseconds != 0) {
 89→            append(t)
 90→            append(when {
 91→                seconds != 0 -> seconds * sign
 92→                nanoseconds * sign < 0 -> "-0"
 93→                else -> "0"
 94→            })
 95→            if (nanoseconds != 0) append('.').append((nanoseconds.absoluteValue).toString().padStart(9, '0'))
 96→            append('S')
 97→        }
 98→
 99→        if (length == 1) append("0D")
100→    }
101→
102→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
103→        if (this === other) return true
104→        if (other !is DateTimePeriod) return false
105→
106→        if (totalMonths != other.totalMonths) return false
107→        if (days != other.days) return false
108→        if (totalNanoseconds != other.totalNanoseconds) return false
109→
110→        return true
111→    }
112→
113→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
114→        var result = totalMonths
115→        result = 31 * result + days
116→        result = 31 * result + totalNanoseconds.hashCode()
117→        return result
118→    }
119→
120→    public companion object {
121→        /**
122→         * Parses a ISO-8601 duration string as a [DateTimePeriod].
123→         * If the time components are absent or equal to zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
124→         *
125→         * Additionally, we support the `W` signifier to represent weeks.
126→         *
127→         * Examples of durations in the ISO-8601 format:
128→         * - `P1Y40D` is one year and 40 days
129→         * - `-P1DT1H` is minus (one day and one hour)
130→         * - `P1DT-1H` is one day minus one hour
131→         * - `-PT0.000000001S` is minus one nanosecond
132→         *
133→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [DateTimePeriod] are
134→         * exceeded.
135→         */
136→        public fun parse(text: String): DateTimePeriod {
137→            fun parseException(message: String, position: Int): Nothing =
138→                throw DateTimeFormatException("Parse error at char $position: $message")
139→            val START = 0
140→            val AFTER_P = 1
141→            val AFTER_YEAR = 2
142→            val AFTER_MONTH = 3
143→            val AFTER_WEEK = 4
144→            val AFTER_DAY = 5
145→            val AFTER_T = 6
146→            val AFTER_HOUR = 7
147→            val AFTER_MINUTE = 8
148→            val AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO = 9
149→
150→            var state = START
151→            // next unread character
152→            var i = 0
153→            var sign = 1
154→            var years = 0
155→            var months = 0
156→            var weeks = 0
157→            var days = 0
158→            var hours = 0
159→            var minutes = 0
160→            var seconds = 0
161→            var nanoseconds = 0
162→            while (true) {
163→                if (i >= text.length) {
164→                    if (state == START)
165→                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; 'P' designator is required", i)
166→                    if (state == AFTER_T)
167→                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; at least one time component is required after 'T'", i)
168→                    val daysTotal = when (val n = days.toLong() + weeks * 7) {
169→                        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> n.toInt()
170→                        else -> parseException("The total number of days under 'D' and 'W' designators should fit into an Int", 0)
171→                    }
172→                    return DateTimePeriod(years, months, daysTotal, hours, minutes, seconds, nanoseconds.toLong())
173→                }
174→                if (state == START) {
175→                    if (i + 1 >= text.length && (text[i] == '+' || text[i] == '-'))
176→                        parseException("Unexpected end of string; 'P' designator is required", i)
177→                    when (text[i]) {
178→                        '+', '-' -> {
179→                            if (text[i] == '-')
180→                                sign = -1
181→                            if (text[i + 1] != 'P')
182→                                parseException("Expected 'P', got '${text[i + 1]}'", i + 1)
183→                            i += 2
184→                        }
185→                        'P' -> { i += 1 }
186→                        else -> parseException("Expected '+', '-', 'P', got '${text[i]}'", i)
187→                    }
188→                    state = AFTER_P
189→                    continue
190→                }
191→                var localSign = sign
192→                val iStart = i
193→                when (text[i]) {
194→                    '+', '-' -> {
195→                        if (text[i] == '-') localSign *= -1
196→                        i += 1
197→                        if (i >= text.length || text[i] !in '0'..'9')
198→                            parseException("A number expected after '${text[i]}'", i)
199→                    }
200→                    in '0'..'9' -> { }
201→                    'T' -> {
202→                        if (state >= AFTER_T)
203→                            parseException("Only one 'T' designator is allowed", i)
204→                        state = AFTER_T
205→                        i += 1
206→                        continue
207→                    }
208→                }
209→                var number = 0L
210→                while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9') {
211→                    try {
212→                        number = safeAdd(safeMultiply(number, 10), (text[i] - '0').toLong())
213→                    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
214→                        parseException("The number is too large", iStart)
215→                    }
216→                    i += 1
217→                }
218→                number *= localSign
219→                if (i == text.length)
220→                    parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
221→                val wrongOrder = "Wrong component order: should be 'Y', 'M', 'W', 'D', then designator 'T', then 'H', 'M', 'S'"
222→                fun Long.toIntThrowing(component: Char): Int {
223→                    if (this < Int.MIN_VALUE || this > Int.MAX_VALUE)
224→                        parseException("Value $this does not fit into an Int, which is required for component '$component'", iStart)
225→                    return toInt()
226→                }
227→                when (text[i].uppercaseChar()) {
228→                    'Y' -> {
229→                        if (state >= AFTER_YEAR)
230→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
231→                        state = AFTER_YEAR
232→                        years = number.toIntThrowing('Y')
233→                    }
234→                    'M' -> {
235→                        if (state >= AFTER_T) {
236→                            // Minutes
237→                            if (state >= AFTER_MINUTE)
238→                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
239→                            state = AFTER_MINUTE
240→                            minutes = number.toIntThrowing('M')
241→                        } else {
242→                            // Months
243→                            if (state >= AFTER_MONTH)
244→                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
245→                            state = AFTER_MONTH
246→                            months = number.toIntThrowing('M')
247→                        }
248→                    }
249→                    'W' -> {
250→                        if (state >= AFTER_WEEK)
251→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
252→                        state = AFTER_WEEK
253→                        weeks = number.toIntThrowing('W')
254→                    }
255→                    'D' -> {
256→                        if (state >= AFTER_DAY)
257→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
258→                        state = AFTER_DAY
259→                        days = number.toIntThrowing('D')
260→                    }
261→                    'H' -> {
262→                        if (state >= AFTER_HOUR || state < AFTER_T)
263→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
264→                        state = AFTER_HOUR
265→                        hours = number.toIntThrowing('H')
266→                    }
267→                    'S' -> {
268→                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
269→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
270→                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
271→                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
272→                    }
273→                    '.', ',' -> {
274→                        i += 1
275→                        if (i >= text.length)
276→                            parseException("Expected designator 'S' after ${text[i - 1]}", i)
277→                        val iStartFraction = i
278→                        while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9')
279→                            i += 1
280→                        val fractionLength = i - iStartFraction
281→                        if (fractionLength > 9)
282→                            parseException("Only the nanosecond fractions of a second are supported", iStartFraction)
283→                        val fractionalPart = text.substring(iStartFraction, i) + "0".repeat(9 - fractionLength)
284→                        nanoseconds = fractionalPart.toInt(10) * localSign
285→                        if (text[i] != 'S')
286→                            parseException("Expected the 'S' designator after a fraction", i)
287→                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
288→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
289→                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
290→                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
291→                    }
292→                    else -> parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
293→                }
294→                i += 1
295→           }
296→        }
297→    }
298→}

300→/**
301→ * @suppress
302→ */
303→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("DateTimePeriod.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
304→public fun String.toDateTimePeriod(): DateTimePeriod = DateTimePeriod.parse(this)
305→
306→/**
307→ * A special case of [DateTimePeriod] that only stores date components and has all time components equal to zero.
308→ *
309→ * A `DatePeriod` is automatically returned from all constructor functions for [DateTimePeriod] if it turns out that
310→ * the time components are zero.
311→ *
312→ * `DatePeriod` values are used in operations on [LocalDates][LocalDate] and are returned from operations on [LocalDates][LocalDate],
313→ * but they also can be passed anywhere a [DateTimePeriod] is expected.
314→ */
315→@Serializable(with = DatePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
316→public class DatePeriod internal constructor(
317→    internal override val totalMonths: Int,
318→    override val days: Int,
319→) : DateTimePeriod() {
320→    public constructor(years: Int = 0, months: Int = 0, days: Int = 0): this(totalMonths(years, months), days)
321→    // avoiding excessive computations
322→    /** The number of whole hours in this period. Always equal to zero. */
323→    override val hours: Int get() = 0
324→
325→    /** The number of whole minutes in this period. Always equal to zero. */
326→    override val minutes: Int get() = 0
327→
328→    /** The number of whole seconds in this period. Always equal to zero. */
329→    override val seconds: Int get() = 0
330→
331→    /** The number of nanoseconds in this period. Always equal to zero. */
332→    override val nanoseconds: Int get() = 0
333→    internal override val totalNanoseconds: Long get() = 0
334→
335→    public companion object {
336→        /**
337→         * Parses the ISO-8601 duration representation as a [DatePeriod].
338→         *
339→         * This function is equivalent to [DateTimePeriod.parse], but will fail if any of the time components are not
340→         * zero.
341→         *
342→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed, the boundaries of [DatePeriod] are exceeded,
343→         * or any time components are not zero.
344→         *
345→         * @see DateTimePeriod.parse
346→         */
347→        public fun parse(text: String): DatePeriod =
348→            when (val period = DateTimePeriod.parse(text)) {
349→                is DatePeriod -> period
350→                else -> throw DateTimeFormatException("Period $period (parsed from string $text) is not date-based")
351→            }
352→    }
353→}
354→
355→/**
356→ * @suppress
357→ */
358→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("DatePeriod.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
359→public fun String.toDatePeriod(): DatePeriod = DatePeriod.parse(this)
360→
361→private class DateTimePeriodImpl(
362→    internal override val totalMonths: Int,
363→    override val days: Int,
364→    internal override val totalNanoseconds: Long,
365→) : DateTimePeriod()
366→
367→// TODO: these calculations fit in a JS Number. Possible to do an expect/actual here.
368→private fun totalMonths(years: Int, months: Int): Int =
369→    when (val totalMonths = years.toLong() * 12 + months.toLong()) {
370→        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> totalMonths.toInt()
371→        else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of months in $years years and $months months overflows an Int")
372→    }
373→
374→private fun totalNanoseconds(hours: Int, minutes: Int, seconds: Int, nanoseconds: Long): Long {
375→    val totalMinutes: Long = hours.toLong() * 60 + minutes
376→    // absolute value at most 61 * Int.MAX_VALUE
377→    val totalMinutesAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutes * 60
378→    // absolute value at most 61 * 60 * Int.MAX_VALUE < 64 * 64 * 2^31 = 2^43
379→    val minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutesAsSeconds + nanoseconds / NANOS_PER_ONE
380→    // absolute value at most 2^43 + 2^63 / 10^9 < 2^43 + 2^34 < 2^44
381→    val totalSeconds = minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds + seconds
382→    // absolute value at most 2^44 + 2^31 < 2^45
383→    return try {
384→        multiplyAndAdd(totalSeconds, 1_000_000_000, nanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE)
385→    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
386→        throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of nanoseconds in $hours hours, $minutes minutes, $seconds seconds, and $nanoseconds nanoseconds overflows a Long")
387→    }
388→}
389→
390→internal fun buildDateTimePeriod(totalMonths: Int = 0, days: Int = 0, totalNanoseconds: Long): DateTimePeriod =
391→    if (totalNanoseconds != 0L)
392→        DateTimePeriodImpl(totalMonths, days, totalNanoseconds)
393→    else
394→        DatePeriod(totalMonths, days)
395→
396→/**
397→ * Constructs a new [DateTimePeriod]. If all the time components are zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
398→ *
399→ * It is recommended to always explicitly name the arguments when constructing this manually,
400→ * like `DateTimePeriod(years = 1, months = 12)`.
401→ *
402→ * The passed numbers are not stored as is but are normalized instead for human readability, so, for example,
403→ * `DateTimePeriod(months = 24)` becomes `DateTimePeriod(years = 2)`.
404→ *
405→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the total number of months in [years] and [months] overflows an [Int].
406→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the total number of months in [hours], [minutes], [seconds] and [nanoseconds]
407→ * overflows a [Long].
408→ */
409→public fun DateTimePeriod(
410→    years: Int = 0,
411→    months: Int = 0,
412→    days: Int = 0,
413→    hours: Int = 0,
414→    minutes: Int = 0,
415→    seconds: Int = 0,
416→    nanoseconds: Long = 0
417→): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(totalMonths(years, months), days,
418→    totalNanoseconds(hours, minutes, seconds, nanoseconds))
419→
420→/**
421→ * Constructs a [DateTimePeriod] from a [Duration].
422→ *
423→ * If the duration value is too big to be represented as a [Long] number of nanoseconds,
424→ * the result will be [Long.MAX_VALUE] nanoseconds.
425→ */
426→// TODO: maybe it's more consistent to throw here on overflow?
427→public fun Duration.toDateTimePeriod(): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(totalNanoseconds = inWholeNanoseconds)
428→
429→/**
430→ * Adds two [DateTimePeriod] instances.
431→ *
432→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow happens.
433→ */
434→public operator fun DateTimePeriod.plus(other: DateTimePeriod): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(
435→    safeAdd(totalMonths, other.totalMonths),
436→    safeAdd(days, other.days),
437→    safeAdd(totalNanoseconds, other.totalNanoseconds),
438→)
439→
440→/**
441→ * Adds two [DatePeriod] instances.
442→ *
443→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow happens.
444→ */
445→public operator fun DatePeriod.plus(other: DatePeriod): DatePeriod = DatePeriod(
446→    safeAdd(totalMonths, other.totalMonths),
447→    safeAdd(days, other.days),
448→)

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2020 JetBrains s.r.o.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer
 11→import kotlin.math.*
 12→import kotlin.time.Duration
 13→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 14→
 15→/**
 16→ * A difference between two [instants][Instant], decomposed into date and time components.
 17→ *
 18→ * The date components are: [years], [months], [days].
 19→ *
 20→ * The time components are: [hours], [minutes], [seconds], [nanoseconds].
 21→ *
 22→ * A `DateTimePeriod` can be constructed using the same-named constructor function,
 23→ * [parsed][DateTimePeriod.parse] from a string, or returned as the result of instant arithmetic operations (see [Instant.periodUntil]).
 24→ * All these functions can return a [DatePeriod] value, which is a subtype of `DateTimePeriod`,
 25→ * a special case that only stores date components, if all time components of the result happen to be zero.
 26→ */
 27→@Serializable(with = DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
 28→// TODO: could be error-prone without explicitly named params
 29→public sealed class DateTimePeriod {
 30→    internal abstract val totalMonths: Int
 31→
 32→    /**
 33→     * The number of calendar days.
 34→     *
 35→     * Note that a calendar day is not identical to 24 hours, see [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
 36→     */
 37→    public abstract val days: Int
 38→    internal abstract val totalNanoseconds: Long
 39→
 40→    /**
 41→     * The number of whole years.
 42→     */
 43→    public val years: Int get() = totalMonths / 12
 44→
 45→    /**
 46→     * The number of months in this period that don't form a whole year, so this value is always in `(-11..11)`.
 47→     */
 48→    public val months: Int get() = totalMonths % 12
 49→
 50→    /**
 51→     * The number of whole hours in this period.
 52→     */
 53→    public open val hours: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds / 3_600_000_000_000).toInt()
 54→
 55→    /**
 56→     * The number of whole minutes in this period that don't form a whole hour, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
 57→     */
 58→    public open val minutes: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 3_600_000_000_000) / 60_000_000_000).toInt()
 59→
 60→    /**
 61→     * The number of whole seconds in this period that don't form a whole minute, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
 62→     */
 63→    public open val seconds: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 60_000_000_000) / NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
 64→
 65→    /**
 66→     * The number of whole nanoseconds in this period that don't form a whole second, so this value is always in
 67→     * `(-999_999_999..999_999_999)`.
 68→     */
 69→    public open val nanoseconds: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
 70→
 71→    private fun allNonpositive() =
 72→        totalMonths <= 0 && days <= 0 && totalNanoseconds <= 0 && (totalMonths or days != 0 || totalNanoseconds != 0L)
 73→
 74→    /**
 75→     * Converts this period to the ISO-8601 string representation for durations.
 76→     *
 77→     * @see DateTimePeriod.parse
 78→     */
 79→    override fun toString(): String = buildString {
 80→        val sign = if (allNonpositive()) { append('-'); -1 } else 1
 81→        append('P')
 82→        if (years != 0) append(years * sign).append('Y')
 83→        if (months != 0) append(months * sign).append('M')
 84→        if (days != 0) append(days * sign).append('D')
 85→        var t = "T"
 86→        if (hours != 0) append(t).append(hours * sign).append('H').also { t = "" }
 87→        if (minutes != 0) append(t).append(minutes * sign).append('M').also { t = "" }
 88→        if (seconds or nanoseconds != 0) {
 89→            append(t)
 90→            append(when {
 91→                seconds != 0 -> seconds * sign
 92→                nanoseconds * sign < 0 -> "-0"
 93→                else -> "0"
 94→            })
 95→            if (nanoseconds != 0) append('.').append((nanoseconds.absoluteValue).toString().padStart(9, '0'))
 96→            append('S')
 97→        }
 98→
 99→        if (length == 1) append("0D")
100→    }
101→
102→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
103→        if (this === other) return true
104→        if (other !is DateTimePeriod) return false
105→
106→        if (totalMonths != other.totalMonths) return false
107→        if (days != other.days) return false
108→        if (totalNanoseconds != other.totalNanoseconds) return false
109→
110→        return true
111→    }
112→
113→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
114→        var result = totalMonths
115→        result = 31 * result + days
116→        result = 31 * result + totalNanoseconds.hashCode()
117→        return result
118→    }
119→
120→    public companion object {
121→        /**
122→         * Parses a ISO-8601 duration string as a [DateTimePeriod].
123→         * If the time components are absent or equal to zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
124→         *
125→         * Additionally, we support the `W` signifier to represent weeks.
126→         *
127→         * Examples of durations in the ISO-8601 format:
128→         * - `P1Y40D` is one year and 40 days
129→         * - `-P1DT1H` is minus (one day and one hour)
130→         * - `P1DT-1H` is one day minus one hour
131→         * - `-PT0.000000001S` is minus one nanosecond
132→         *
133→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [DateTimePeriod] are
134→         * exceeded.
135→         */
136→        public fun parse(text: String): DateTimePeriod {
137→            fun parseException(message: String, position: Int): Nothing =
138→                throw DateTimeFormatException("Parse error at char $position: $message")
139→            val START = 0
140→            val AFTER_P = 1
141→            val AFTER_YEAR = 2
142→            val AFTER_MONTH = 3
143→            val AFTER_WEEK = 4
144→            val AFTER_DAY = 5
145→            val AFTER_T = 6
146→            val AFTER_HOUR = 7
147→            val AFTER_MINUTE = 8
148→            val AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO = 9
149→
150→            var state = START
151→            // next unread character
152→            var i = 0
153→            var sign = 1
154→            var years = 0
155→            var months = 0
156→            var weeks = 0
157→            var days = 0
158→            var hours = 0
159→            var minutes = 0
160→            var seconds = 0
161→            var nanoseconds = 0
162→            while (true) {
163→                if (i >= text.length) {
164→                    if (state == START)
165→                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; 'P' designator is required", i)
166→                    if (state == AFTER_T)
167→                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; at least one time component is required after 'T'", i)
168→                    val daysTotal = when (val n = days.toLong() + weeks * 7) {
169→                        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> n.toInt()
170→                        else -> parseException("The total number of days under 'D' and 'W' designators should fit into an Int", 0)
171→                    }
172→                    return DateTimePeriod(years, months, daysTotal, hours, minutes, seconds, nanoseconds.toLong())
173→                }
174→                if (state == START) {
175→                    if (i + 1 >= text.length && (text[i] == '+' || text[i] == '-'))
176→                        parseException("Unexpected end of string; 'P' designator is required", i)
177→                    when (text[i]) {
178→                        '+', '-' -> {
179→                            if (text[i] == '-')
180→                                sign = -1
181→                            if (text[i + 1] != 'P')
182→                                parseException("Expected 'P', got '${text[i + 1]}'", i + 1)
183→                            i += 2
184→                        }
185→                        'P' -> { i += 1 }
186→                        else -> parseException("Expected '+', '-', 'P', got '${text[i]}'", i)
187→                    }
188→                    state = AFTER_P
189→                    continue
190→                }
191→                var localSign = sign
192→                val iStart = i
193→                when (text[i]) {
194→                    '+', '-' -> {
195→                        if (text[i] == '-') localSign *= -1
196→                        i += 1
197→                        if (i >= text.length || text[i] !in '0'..'9')
198→                            parseException("A number expected after '${text[i]}'", i)
199→                    }
200→                    in '0'..'9' -> { }
201→                    'T' -> {
202→                        if (state >= AFTER_T)
203→                            parseException("Only one 'T' designator is allowed", i)
204→                        state = AFTER_T
205→                        i += 1
206→                        continue
207→                    }
208→                }
209→                var number = 0L
210→                while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9') {
211→                    try {
212→                        number = safeAdd(safeMultiply(number, 10), (text[i] - '0').toLong())
213→                    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
214→                        parseException("The number is too large", iStart)
215→                    }
216→                    i += 1
217→                }
218→                number *= localSign
219→                if (i == text.length)
220→                    parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
221→                val wrongOrder = "Wrong component order: should be 'Y', 'M', 'W', 'D', then designator 'T', then 'H', 'M', 'S'"
222→                fun Long.toIntThrowing(component: Char): Int {
223→                    if (this < Int.MIN_VALUE || this > Int.MAX_VALUE)
224→                        parseException("Value $this does not fit into an Int, which is required for component '$component'", iStart)
225→                    return toInt()
226→                }
227→                when (text[i].uppercaseChar()) {
228→                    'Y' -> {
229→                        if (state >= AFTER_YEAR)
230→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
231→                        state = AFTER_YEAR
232→                        years = number.toIntThrowing('Y')
233→                    }
234→                    'M' -> {
235→                        if (state >= AFTER_T) {
236→                            // Minutes
237→                            if (state >= AFTER_MINUTE)
238→                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
239→                            state = AFTER_MINUTE
240→                            minutes = number.toIntThrowing('M')
241→                        } else {
242→                            // Months
243→                            if (state >= AFTER_MONTH)
244→                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
245→                            state = AFTER_MONTH
246→                            months = number.toIntThrowing('M')
247→                        }
248→                    }
249→                    'W' -> {
250→                        if (state >= AFTER_WEEK)
251→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
252→                        state = AFTER_WEEK
253→                        weeks = number.toIntThrowing('W')
254→                    }
255→                    'D' -> {
256→                        if (state >= AFTER_DAY)
257→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
258→                        state = AFTER_DAY
259→                        days = number.toIntThrowing('D')
260→                    }
261→                    'H' -> {
262→                        if (state >= AFTER_HOUR || state < AFTER_T)
263→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
264→                        state = AFTER_HOUR
265→                        hours = number.toIntThrowing('H')
266→                    }
267→                    'S' -> {
268→                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
269→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
270→                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
271→                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
272→                    }
273→                    '.', ',' -> {
274→                        i += 1
275→                        if (i >= text.length)
276→                            parseException("Expected designator 'S' after ${text[i - 1]}", i)
277→                        val iStartFraction = i
278→                        while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9')
279→                            i += 1
280→                        val fractionLength = i - iStartFraction
281→                        if (fractionLength > 9)
282→                            parseException("Only the nanosecond fractions of a second are supported", iStartFraction)
283→                        val fractionalPart = text.substring(iStartFraction, i) + "0".repeat(9 - fractionLength)
284→                        nanoseconds = fractionalPart.toInt(10) * localSign
285→                        if (text[i] != 'S')
286→                            parseException("Expected the 'S' designator after a fraction", i)
287→                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
288→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
289→                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
290→                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
291→                    }
292→                    else -> parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
293→                }
294→                i += 1
295→           }
296→        }
297→    }
298→}

300→/**
301→ * @suppress
302→ */
303→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("DateTimePeriod.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
304→public fun String.toDateTimePeriod(): DateTimePeriod = DateTimePeriod.parse(this)

306→/**
307→ * A special case of [DateTimePeriod] that only stores date components and has all time components equal to zero.
308→ *
309→ * A `DatePeriod` is automatically returned from all constructor functions for [DateTimePeriod] if it turns out that
310→ * the time components are zero.
311→ *
312→ * `DatePeriod` values are used in operations on [LocalDates][LocalDate] and are returned from operations on [LocalDates][LocalDate],
313→ * but they also can be passed anywhere a [DateTimePeriod] is expected.
314→ */
315→@Serializable(with = DatePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
316→public class DatePeriod internal constructor(
317→    internal override val totalMonths: Int,
318→    override val days: Int,
319→) : DateTimePeriod() {
320→    public constructor(years: Int = 0, months: Int = 0, days: Int = 0): this(totalMonths(years, months), days)
321→    // avoiding excessive computations
322→    /** The number of whole hours in this period. Always equal to zero. */
323→    override val hours: Int get() = 0
324→
325→    /** The number of whole minutes in this period. Always equal to zero. */
326→    override val minutes: Int get() = 0
327→
328→    /** The number of whole seconds in this period. Always equal to zero. */
329→    override val seconds: Int get() = 0
330→
331→    /** The number of nanoseconds in this period. Always equal to zero. */
332→    override val nanoseconds: Int get() = 0
333→    internal override val totalNanoseconds: Long get() = 0
334→
335→    public companion object {
336→        /**
337→         * Parses the ISO-8601 duration representation as a [DatePeriod].
338→         *
339→         * This function is equivalent to [DateTimePeriod.parse], but will fail if any of the time components are not
340→         * zero.
341→         *
342→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed, the boundaries of [DatePeriod] are exceeded,
343→         * or any time components are not zero.
344→         *
345→         * @see DateTimePeriod.parse
346→         */
347→        public fun parse(text: String): DatePeriod =
348→            when (val period = DateTimePeriod.parse(text)) {
349→                is DatePeriod -> period
350→                else -> throw DateTimeFormatException("Period $period (parsed from string $text) is not date-based")
351→            }
352→    }
353→}
354→
355→/**
356→ * @suppress
357→ */
358→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("DatePeriod.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
359→public fun String.toDatePeriod(): DatePeriod = DatePeriod.parse(this)

361→private class DateTimePeriodImpl(
362→    internal override val totalMonths: Int,
363→    override val days: Int,
364→    internal override val totalNanoseconds: Long,
365→) : DateTimePeriod()
366→
367→// TODO: these calculations fit in a JS Number. Possible to do an expect/actual here.
368→private fun totalMonths(years: Int, months: Int): Int =
369→    when (val totalMonths = years.toLong() * 12 + months.toLong()) {
370→        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> totalMonths.toInt()
371→        else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of months in $years years and $months months overflows an Int")
372→    }
373→
374→private fun totalNanoseconds(hours: Int, minutes: Int, seconds: Int, nanoseconds: Long): Long {
375→    val totalMinutes: Long = hours.toLong() * 60 + minutes
376→    // absolute value at most 61 * Int.MAX_VALUE
377→    val totalMinutesAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutes * 60
378→    // absolute value at most 61 * 60 * Int.MAX_VALUE < 64 * 64 * 2^31 = 2^43
379→    val minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutesAsSeconds + nanoseconds / NANOS_PER_ONE
380→    // absolute value at most 2^43 + 2^63 / 10^9 < 2^43 + 2^34 < 2^44
381→    val totalSeconds = minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds + seconds
382→    // absolute value at most 2^44 + 2^31 < 2^45
383→    return try {
384→        multiplyAndAdd(totalSeconds, 1_000_000_000, nanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE)
385→    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
386→        throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of nanoseconds in $hours hours, $minutes minutes, $seconds seconds, and $nanoseconds nanoseconds overflows a Long")
387→    }
388→}
389→
390→internal fun buildDateTimePeriod(totalMonths: Int = 0, days: Int = 0, totalNanoseconds: Long): DateTimePeriod =
391→    if (totalNanoseconds != 0L)
392→        DateTimePeriodImpl(totalMonths, days, totalNanoseconds)
393→    else
394→        DatePeriod(totalMonths, days)
395→
396→/**
397→ * Constructs a new [DateTimePeriod]. If all the time components are zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
398→ *
399→ * It is recommended to always explicitly name the arguments when constructing this manually,
400→ * like `DateTimePeriod(years = 1, months = 12)`.
401→ *
402→ * The passed numbers are not stored as is but are normalized instead for human readability, so, for example,
403→ * `DateTimePeriod(months = 24)` becomes `DateTimePeriod(years = 2)`.
404→ *
405→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the total number of months in [years] and [months] overflows an [Int].
406→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the total number of months in [hours], [minutes], [seconds] and [nanoseconds]
407→ * overflows a [Long].
408→ */
409→public fun DateTimePeriod(
410→    years: Int = 0,
411→    months: Int = 0,
412→    days: Int = 0,
413→    hours: Int = 0,
414→    minutes: Int = 0,
415→    seconds: Int = 0,
416→    nanoseconds: Long = 0
417→): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(totalMonths(years, months), days,
418→    totalNanoseconds(hours, minutes, seconds, nanoseconds))
419→
420→/**
421→ * Constructs a [DateTimePeriod] from a [Duration].
422→ *
423→ * If the duration value is too big to be represented as a [Long] number of nanoseconds,
424→ * the result will be [Long.MAX_VALUE] nanoseconds.
425→ */
426→// TODO: maybe it's more consistent to throw here on overflow?
427→public fun Duration.toDateTimePeriod(): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(totalNanoseconds = inWholeNanoseconds)
428→
429→/**
430→ * Adds two [DateTimePeriod] instances.
431→ *
432→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow happens.
433→ */
434→public operator fun DateTimePeriod.plus(other: DateTimePeriod): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(
435→    safeAdd(totalMonths, other.totalMonths),
436→    safeAdd(days, other.days),
437→    safeAdd(totalNanoseconds, other.totalNanoseconds),
438→)
439→
440→/**
441→ * Adds two [DatePeriod] instances.
442→ *
443→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow happens.
444→ */
445→public operator fun DatePeriod.plus(other: DatePeriod): DatePeriod = DatePeriod(
446→    safeAdd(totalMonths, other.totalMonths),
447→    safeAdd(days, other.days),
448→)

  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.*
 10→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11→import kotlin.time.*
 12→import kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.nanoseconds
 13→
 14→/**
 15→ * A unit for measuring time.
 16→ *
 17→ * See the predefined constants for time units, like [DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND], [DateTimeUnit.DAY],
 18→ * [DateTimeUnit.MONTH], and others.
 19→ *
 20→ * Two ways are provided to create custom [DateTimeUnit] instances:
 21→ * - By multiplying an existing unit on the right by an integer scalar: for example, `DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND * 10`.
 22→ * - By constructing an instance manually with [TimeBased], [DayBased], or [MonthBased]: for example,
 23→ *   `TimeBased(nanoseconds = 10)`.
 24→ *
 25→ * Note that a calendar day is not considered identical to 24 hours. See [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for a discussion.
 26→ */
 27→@Serializable(with = DateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
 28→public sealed class DateTimeUnit {
 29→
 30→    /** Produces a date-time unit that is a multiple of this unit times the specified integer [scalar] value. */
 31→    public abstract operator fun times(scalar: Int): DateTimeUnit
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * A date-time unit that has the precise time duration.
 35→     *
 36→     * Such units are independent of the time zone.
 37→     * Any such unit can be represented as some fixed number of nanoseconds.
 38→     */
 39→    @Serializable(with = TimeBasedDateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
 40→    public class TimeBased(
 41→        /**
 42→         * The length of this unit in nanoseconds.
 43→         */
 44→        public val nanoseconds: Long
 45→    ) : DateTimeUnit() {
 46→        private val unitName: String
 47→        private val unitScale: Long
 48→
 49→        init {
 50→            require(nanoseconds > 0) { "Unit duration must be positive, but was $nanoseconds ns." }
 51→            // find a concise string representation for the unit with this duration
 52→            when {
 53→                nanoseconds % 3600_000_000_000 == 0L -> {
 54→                    unitName = "HOUR"
 55→                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 3600_000_000_000
 56→                }
 57→                nanoseconds % 60_000_000_000 == 0L -> {
 58→                    unitName = "MINUTE"
 59→                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 60_000_000_000
 60→                }
 61→                nanoseconds % 1_000_000_000 == 0L -> {
 62→                    unitName = "SECOND"
 63→                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 1_000_000_000
 64→                }
 65→                nanoseconds % 1_000_000 == 0L -> {
 66→                    unitName = "MILLISECOND"
 67→                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 1_000_000
 68→                }
 69→                nanoseconds % 1_000 == 0L -> {
 70→                    unitName = "MICROSECOND"
 71→                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 1_000
 72→                }
 73→                else -> {
 74→                    unitName = "NANOSECOND"
 75→                    unitScale = nanoseconds
 76→                }
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→
 80→        override fun times(scalar: Int): TimeBased = TimeBased(safeMultiply(nanoseconds, scalar.toLong()))
 81→
 82→        /**
 83→         * The length of this unit as a [Duration].
 84→         */
 85→        public val duration: Duration
 86→            get() = nanoseconds.nanoseconds
 87→
 88→        override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 89→            this === other || (other is TimeBased && this.nanoseconds == other.nanoseconds)
 90→
 91→        override fun hashCode(): Int = nanoseconds.toInt() xor (nanoseconds shr Int.SIZE_BITS).toInt()
 92→
 93→        override fun toString(): String = formatToString(unitScale, unitName)
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    /**
 97→     * A date-time unit equal to some number of days or months.
 98→     *
 99→     * Operations involving `DateBased` units are performed on dates. The same operations on [Instants][Instant]
100→     * require a [TimeZone] to find the corresponding [LocalDateTimes][LocalDateTime] first to perform
101→     * the operation with the date component of these `LocalDateTime` values.
102→     */
103→    @Serializable(with = DateBasedDateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
104→    public sealed class DateBased : DateTimeUnit() {
105→        @Suppress("TOPLEVEL_TYPEALIASES_ONLY")
106→        @Deprecated("Use DateTimeUnit.DayBased", ReplaceWith("DateTimeUnit.DayBased", "kotlinx.datetime.DateTimeUnit"))
107→        public typealias DayBased = DateTimeUnit.DayBased
108→        @Suppress("TOPLEVEL_TYPEALIASES_ONLY")
109→        @Deprecated("Use DateTimeUnit.MonthBased", ReplaceWith("DateTimeUnit.MonthBased", "kotlinx.datetime.DateTimeUnit"))
110→        public typealias MonthBased = DateTimeUnit.MonthBased
111→    }
112→
113→    /**
114→     * A date-time unit equal to some number of calendar days.
115→     *
116→     * A calendar day is not considered identical to 24 hours, thus a `DayBased`-unit cannot be expressed as a multiple of some [TimeBased]-unit.
117→     *
118→     * The reason lies in time zone transitions, because of which some days can be 23 or 25 hours.
119→     * For example, we say that exactly a whole day has passed between `2019-10-27T02:59` and `2019-10-28T02:59`
120→     * in Berlin, despite the fact that the clocks were turned back one hour, so there are, in fact, 25 hours
121→     * between the two date-times.
122→     */
123→    @Serializable(with = DayBasedDateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
124→    public class DayBased(
125→        /**
126→         * The length of this unit in days.
127→         */
128→        public val days: Int
129→    ) : DateBased() {
130→        init {
131→            require(days > 0) { "Unit duration must be positive, but was $days days." }
132→        }
133→
134→        override fun times(scalar: Int): DateTimeUnit.DayBased = DateTimeUnit.DayBased(safeMultiply(days, scalar))
135→
136→        override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
137→            this === other || (other is DateTimeUnit.DayBased && this.days == other.days)
138→
139→        override fun hashCode(): Int = days xor 0x10000
140→
141→        override fun toString(): String = if (days % 7 == 0)
142→            formatToString(days / 7, "WEEK")
143→        else
144→            formatToString(days, "DAY")
145→    }
146→
147→    /**
148→     * A date-time unit equal to some number of months.
149→     *
150→     * Since different months have different number of days, a `MonthBased`-unit cannot be expressed a multiple of some [DayBased]-unit.
151→     */
152→    @Serializable(with = MonthBasedDateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
153→    public class MonthBased(
154→        /**
155→         * The length of this unit in months.
156→         */
157→        public val months: Int
158→    ) : DateBased() {
159→        init {
160→            require(months > 0) { "Unit duration must be positive, but was $months months." }
161→        }
162→
163→        override fun times(scalar: Int): DateTimeUnit.MonthBased = DateTimeUnit.MonthBased(safeMultiply(months, scalar))
164→
165→        override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
166→            this === other || (other is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased && this.months == other.months)
167→
168→        override fun hashCode(): Int = months xor 0x20000
169→
170→        override fun toString(): String = when {
171→            months % 12_00 == 0 -> formatToString(months / 12_00, "CENTURY")
172→            months % 12 == 0 -> formatToString(months / 12, "YEAR")
173→            months % 3 == 0 -> formatToString(months / 3, "QUARTER")
174→            else -> formatToString(months, "MONTH")
175→        }
176→    }
177→
178→    protected fun formatToString(value: Int, unit: String): String = if (value == 1) unit else "$value-$unit"
179→    protected fun formatToString(value: Long, unit: String): String = if (value == 1L) unit else "$value-$unit"
180→
181→    public companion object {
182→        /**
183→         * A nanosecond, which is `1/1_000_000_000` of a second.
184→         */
185→        public val NANOSECOND: TimeBased = TimeBased(nanoseconds = 1)
186→
187→        /**
188→         * A microsecond, which is `1/1_000_000` of a second, or `1_000` nanoseconds.
189→         */
190→        public val MICROSECOND: TimeBased = NANOSECOND * 1000
191→
192→        /**
193→         * A millisecond, which is `1/1_000` of a second, or `1_000_000` nanoseconds.
194→         */
195→        public val MILLISECOND: TimeBased = MICROSECOND * 1000
196→
197→        /**
198→         * A second.
199→         */
200→        public val SECOND: TimeBased = MILLISECOND * 1000
201→
202→        /**
203→         * A minute, which is 60 seconds.
204→         */
205→        public val MINUTE: TimeBased = SECOND * 60
206→
207→        /**
208→         * An hour, which is 60 minutes, or 3600 seconds.
209→         */
210→        public val HOUR: TimeBased = MINUTE * 60
211→
212→        /**
213→         * A calendar day.
214→         *
215→         * Note that a calendar day is not the same as 24 hours, see [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
216→         */
217→        public val DAY: DayBased = DayBased(days = 1)
218→
219→        /**
220→         * A week, which is 7 [calendar days][DAY].
221→         */
222→        public val WEEK: DayBased = DAY * 7
223→
224→        /**
225→         * A month.
226→         *
227→         * Note that a month doesn't have a constant number of calendar days in it.
228→         */
229→        public val MONTH: MonthBased = MonthBased(months = 1)
230→
231→        /**
232→         * A quarter, which is three [months][MONTH].
233→         */
234→        public val QUARTER: MonthBased = MONTH * 3
235→
236→        /**
237→         * A year, which is 12 [months][MONTH].
238→         */
239→        public val YEAR: MonthBased = MONTH * 12
240→
241→        /**
242→         * A century, which is 100 [years][YEAR], or 1200 [months][MONTH].
243→         */
244→        public val CENTURY: MonthBased = YEAR * 100
245→    }
246→}
